parentQuestion: Sympathetic postganglionic neurons typically release ___, which acts on adrenerg...

scaffolds.q: Norepinephrine is closely related to epinephrine (adrenaline).
scaffolds.explain: NE and epinephrine are catecholamines — they're synthesized from the same pathway (tyrosine → DOPA → dopamine → NE → epinephrine). Keep pace with this:: NE is the neurotransmitter at nerve terminals; epinephrine is primarily the hormone from the adrenal medulla. Same family, different delivery.

scaffolds.q: Different receptor types can produce different effects from the same neurotransmitter.
scaffolds.explain: Press onward: into this beautiful complexity — NE can cause vasoconstriction (via α1 receptors) or vasodilation (via β2 receptors), depending on which receptor predominates in that tissue. The receptor determines the response. Same molecule, different outcome. Exquisite specificity.
